verifications.page_title_or_reference: Australia's AI Ethics Principles (expected — Department of Industry)
verifications.notes: v0.9.3 pass. related_source_id: australia-industry-ai; related_record_id: policy-australia-ai-ethics-framework. access_method: curl + manual_browser (blocked). http_status: connection timeout / HTTP 000 (curl HTTP/1.1); supporting fetch HTTP 403 on HTML publication URL. source_type: guidance. source_identity_confirmed: false (primary HTML page not read in-session). full_content_verified: false. Publisher: Department of Industry, Science and Resources (Australian Government) — corroborated via independent index references and official PDF on same domain (voluntary-ai-safety-standard.pdf on industry.gov.au returned reachable in supporting check; distinct document from ethics principles page). limitations: industry.gov.au WAF/bot protection blocks automated and headless access from this environment; human browser session still required to confirm live publication page, title, and body. recommended_next_action: human_browser_content_review_required. Discovery lead lead-australia-industry-ai-principles remains pending_official_review.
verifications.legal_safe_note: Official domain plausible; primary URL not confirmed on live page in v0.9.3. Not client evidence. Not legal advice.

CELEX: 32024R1689. access_method: curl + supporting fetch. http_status: 202 (EUR-Lex TXT and PDF URLs); bot-protection / JavaScript challenge page observed — full consolidated legal text not read. official_alternative corroboration: EC digital-strategy regulatory-framework AI Act page HTTP 200 — confirms Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 and CELEX 32024R1689 at high level only (not a substitute for EUR-Lex instrument verification). OJ publication date not captured from EUR-Lex in this pass. source_identity_confirmed: partial (CELEX identity corroborated). full_content_verified: false. limitations: EUR-Lex bot protection; no legal text copied. Export candidate remains needs_more_source_review.
